channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Schools nationwide brace for Trump, including measures compelling teachers not to cooperate with ICE
item.description: K-12 school districts, universities and state-level education leaders in the U.S. are drawing up plans to protect students from Trump's immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Winter weather limits North Carolina governor's inauguration to TV speech
item.description: North Carolina's new governor is planning to give his inauguration speech indoors in a televised address after the outdoor public ceremony was canceled due to forecasts for snow, sleet and freezing rain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Panama Canal administrator pushes back against Trump's assertions of Chinese meddling
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — The administrator of the Panama Canal said Friday that the vital waterway will remain in Panamanian hands and open to commerce from all countries, rejecting claims by President-elect Donald Trump that the United States should take it over.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden is still considering pardons for people who have been criticized or threatened by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Friday said he was still considering whether to give pardons to people who have been criticized or threatened by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Legislature prepares for new session, rifts between parties show a partisan divide
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature will gavel-in a new session on Jan. 14 at noon, and MPR’s politics team is at the Capitol to cover it all. Coming up Monday at 9 a.m., MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guests talk about the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass faces critical leadership test as questions emerge about wildfire response
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass is facing withering criticism for her response to the firestorm ravaging her city.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi And Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's viral 'Gulf of America' name-change spurs a Texas-sized suggestion: Gulf of Buc-ee's
item.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw, R-Texas, appeared to satirically call for President-elect Donald Trump's name-change plans for the Gulf of Mexico be altered to the "Gulf of Buc-ee's.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump can still vote after sentencing, but can't own a gun and will have to turn over DNA sample
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump doesn't have to go to jail, pay a fine or perform community service as a result of his New York hush money conviction. A judge ended the case Friday with a sentence of an unconditional discharge, closing the case with no punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS extends TPS for Salvadorans, potentially complicating Trump's plans
item.description: The Biden administration on Friday extended a key humanitarian program for El Salvadoran nationals in a move that could complicate the incoming Trump administration’s plans to do away with long-term immigrant protections.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court appears skeptical of blocking US ban on TiKTok: What to know
item.description: The Supreme Court heard TikTok's case to toss out a ban just nine days before it will take effect. The Biden administration defended the measure on national security grounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President-elect Trump appears virtually in court to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is appearing virtually in a New York courtroom to be sentenced for his hush money conviction, 10 days before he is inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a federal law that could force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court seems likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats fume over Alito-Trump call
item.description: Senate Democrats are furious that President-elect Trump has reached out to conservative Justice Samuel Alito amid a push by Trump for the high court to halt his criminal sentencing.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freedom Caucus members to huddle with Trump on strategy
item.description: Members of the House Freedom Caucus say they will make the case for their preferred policies and strategy when they meet with President-elect Trump at Mar-a-Lago on Friday to discuss his ambitious legislative agenda — bringing preferences that at times clash with those of Trump. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7 notable moments between VIPs at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former President Carter’s funeral service at Washington National Cathedral brought together all five living presidents and current and former officials for an event full of notable interactions.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump hosts Republican governors in Florida and talks anew about Greenland and drones
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump hosted 22 of the nation's 27 Republican governors for dinner at his Florida club on Thursday night, looking to help drive the agenda in conservative-run states after he takes office in 11 days. Montana Gov.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A sharply divided Supreme Court has rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The Biden administration succeeds in temporarily blocking a plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration succeeded Thursday in temporarily blocking accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed from entering a guilty plea in a deal that would spare him the risk of execution for al-Qaida’s Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats join Republicans in voting to advance bill to detain migrants accused of crimes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Newly in the minority, Democrats voted with Republicans on Thursday to advance legislation that would require federal authorities to detain unauthorized immigrants who have been accused of certain crimes — signaling that they will try and find spots to work with President-elect Don
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen. J.D. Vance resigns ahead of vice presidential inauguration
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- U.S. Sen. J.D. Vance resigned from his seat in Congress' upper chamber on Thursday ahead of his inauguration as vice president to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. stock markets close to honor former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq will both observe Thursday's national day of mourning in a Wall Street tradition dating back to 1865.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: “It’s presumptuous to say that, but I think yes,” the president said when reporter Susan Page asked if he could have won.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin asks DOJ to clarify scope of president’s military power ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.), the top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee, is urging the Department of Justice (DOJ) to rescind a number of internal opinions dealing with a president’s military powers while asking for clarification on domestic use of America’s forces.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Freedom Caucus holds its fire on Johnson’s strategy for passing Trump agenda
item.description: Members of the conservative House Freedom Caucus are holding their fire despite Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) bucking their preferred strategy for passing President-elect Trump’s agenda, arguing that the ultimate product is more important than the configuration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: GOP sues over timing of special election crucial to Minnesota House power balance
item.description: A lawsuit challenges a decision by Gov. Tim Walz to schedule a Jan. 28 special election after a November winner was deemed ineligible to take office in the House on Jan. 14.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns as Liberal Party leader, spelling end to time in power
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is certified as the 2024 election winner without challenge, in stark contrast to 2021 violence
item.description: Lawmakers convened under heavy security and a snowstorm to certify the vote, with the reading of the electoral results from the states coming swiftly.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
